attributes.description: Alerts when session utilisation (`sessions.count / sessions.limit`) exceeds 80%. Approaching the limit means new connections will be queued or refused.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Session saturation approaching limit

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy session saturation approaching limit

### What fired
`sessions.count / sessions.limit > 0.80` during the window.

### Why it matters
Sessions are bounded by `maxconn` on each entity. Approaching the limit means bursts will be queued (backend) or refused (frontend). Sustained high utilisation predicts imminent capacity problems.

### Triage
1. Compare against request rate growth.
2. Check backend queue depth and latency.

### Remediation
- Raise `maxconn` on the affected entity.
- Add capacity.

### Tuning
- `> 80%` is the documented warning band.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-haproxyreceiver.otel-*
// Session count and limit at frontend/backend/server level
| WHERE haproxy.sessions.count IS NOT NULL AND haproxy.sessions.limit IS NOT NULL
| WHERE haproxy.sessions.limit > 0
| STATS
    max_sessions = MAX(haproxy.sessions.count),
    max_limit = MAX(haproxy.sessions.limit)
  BY haproxy.proxy_name, haproxy.service_name, haproxy.addr
| EVAL utilisation_pct = ROUND(max_sessions * 100.0 / max_limit, 1)
// Alert when utilisation exceeds 80% — adjust for capacity planning
| WHERE utilisation_pct > 80
| EVAL alert_key = CONCAT(TO_STRING(haproxy.proxy_name), "::", TO_STRING(haproxy.service_name), "::", COALESCE(TO_STRING(haproxy.addr), ""))
| SORT utilisation_pct DESC
| LIMIT 50
